mnemonics: SACRED Data: A DBMS makes data Sharing, Abstraction, Control, Redundancy reduction, Efficiency, and Durability sacred.
keyPoints: Reduced Data Redundancy and Improved Consistency
commonMistakes: Assuming a DBMS eliminates all data redundancy (it controls it)
Believing a DBMS is always faster than file systems (complex queries can be slow)
Confusing logical data independence with physical data independence
